sourceScope.sourceTitle: Life Insurance and Annuity Non-Guaranteed Elements
sourceScope.sourceStatusNote: Official adoption text effective March 19, 2018; page 10 is a blank trailing page and should remain boundary context only.

observedSectionWindows.note: Title page, purpose, scope, and initial definitions.
Qualified actuary, board-approved criteria, and non-guaranteed elements.

boundaries: Keep Regulation 210 focused on the non-guaranteed-elements rule only.
Keep AG files and practice notes out of this wave.
Preserve page locators and line references where available because this is a text-based PDF source.
Treat page 10 as blank trailing boundary context and do not treat it as substantive content.
exclusions: No eLearning scripts, slides, quizzes, or app exports.
No raw source files in Git.
No AG files or practice notes in the same wave.

topicMap.label: Opening title, purpose, scope, and definitions
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the title page and the opening purpose/scope/definition material together and stop before the qualification criteria begin.

topicMap.label: Qualified actuary, board-approved criteria, and non-guaranteed elements
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the qualification criteria and non-guaranteed-elements rules together and stop before filing and records begin.

topicMap.label: Filing and records requirements
topicMap.boundaryNote: Keep the filing and records material together and stop before the blank trailing page.

proposedBatches.title: Regulation 210 opening title, purpose, scope, and definitions slice

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the title page, purpose, scope, and opening definitions as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the title page, purpose, scope, and opening definitions as one narrow review-only slice.

proposedBatches.title: Regulation 210 qualified actuary, board-approved criteria, and non-guaranteed elements slice

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the qualification criteria and non-guaranteed-elements rules before the filing and records section begins.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the qualification criteria and non-guaranteed-elements rules before the filing and records section begins.

proposedBatches.title: Regulation 210 filing and records closeout slice

paragraphTarget.notes: Capture the filing and records requirements as the final narrow review-only slice and keep the closing boundary explicit.

proposedBatches.rationale: Capture the filing and records requirements as the final narrow review-only slice and keep the closing boundary explicit.

reviewStandards.regulatoryRequirement: Treat the purpose, scope, requirements, and filing instructions as regulatory text when they direct what the company must do.
reviewStandards.definitionOrTerminology: Treat non-guaranteed elements, qualified actuary, pricing cell, and related terms as terminology when the text explains rather than directs.
reviewStandards.companyOrPrudentEstimateAssumption: Treat anticipated experience factors and reasonableness language as assumption-layer material when they appear.
reviewStandards.governanceOrControlExpectation: Treat board-approved criteria, qualified actuary requirements, and superintendent review language as governance or control expectations when they appear.
reviewStandards.reportingRequirement: Treat filing and annual submission instructions as reporting requirements if they appear.
reviewStandards.documentationExpectation: Treat actuarial memorandum and records retention instructions as documentation expectations if they appear.
reviewStandards.crossReferenceMapping: Treat references to Insurance Regulation 152, business law, policy form review, and other statutory provisions as cross-reference context unless operationalized.
reviewStandards.backgroundContent: Treat the title page and section labels as background unless they impose a requirement.
reviewStandards.boundaryControlWindow: Treat each page window as review-only and do not widen it unless later cleanup explicitly needs that split.
reviewStandards.requiresHumanInterpretation: Treat the non-guaranteed-elements justification language and any dense regulatory text as requiring human review unless the source text is explicit.

currentChecksNeeded: Plan file exists and parses as JSON.
The Regulation 210 plan markdown must exist and carry the expected headings.
Every planned batch must remain review-only by default.
The source posture must stay explicitly active, with the blank trailing page noted as boundary context.
Each planned batch must have a matching batch definition.
The Regulation 210 batch definition file must stay synchronized with the plan.
Page locators and line references should remain visible in the review packet where available.
futureChecksSuggested: Keep the plan portable if later NY-regulation amendments need to be split into a companion wave.
Keep the review-only guardrails visible until the review index and self-review note are created.

operatingNote.citationGuidance: Page locators are the primary anchor and line references should be preserved where available.
operatingNote.boundaryGuidance: The active regulation text should remain visible as a review-only caveat rather than a promotion signal.
operatingNote.appendixGuidance: Keep the filing and records closeout separate so the source ends at a visible boundary.
